strengths: 15+ years of full-stack delivery across TypeScript, Node.js, React, and MongoDB — closely matching the primary stack — with AWS CDK and Kubernetes in production
Current role at re:cinq includes AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code-generator tooling, directly satisfying the AI agent development requirement
Genuine testing culture: authored open-source D-language testing frameworks, ran public TDD/Selenium workshops, and applied TDD and trunk-based development at RIDE Capital
concerns: Nest.js and Vitest are absent from the resume — both are listed as primary stack — leaving the depth of fit with the project's specific framework choices unverified
Kotlin is not mentioned; Java appears only in older, non-web contexts (robotics, mobile device orchestration), leaving the secondary backend requirement largely unmet
No explicit evidence of authentication, authorization, or security-pattern work — a stated required skill — and PostgreSQL is missing despite SQL and MySQL being listed
response.justification: Bogdan presents a strong full-stack profile that aligns closely with the core of the role: TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, AWS, and Kubernetes are all evidenced in recent production work, and his current position at re:cinq — building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling — is a direct match for the increasingly important AI agent development requirement. His testing discipline is unusually well-documented, going beyond usage to authorship and teaching. The gaps that matter most are framework-specific: Nest.js and Vitest are unmentioned, Kotlin is absent, and there is no explicit signal on auth/security patterns or PostgreSQL — all of which appear as required or primary items in the job description. These are gaps to probe rather than disqualifiers, given the overall seniority and breadth of the candidate's background.
